# Copilot instructions for this repository

Purpose: Provide Copilot sessions concise, repository-specific guidance so suggestions are accurate and actionable.

---

## 1) Build, test, and lint commands
- This is a minimal static web project (no package.json, no build system detected).
- Run locally: open index.html in a browser OR serve from a static server:
  - Node: npx http-server ./ -p 8080
  - Python 3: python -m http.server 8000
- No test runner or linter configured. If tests are added with Jest, a single test can be run with:
  - npx jest path/to/test-file.test.js
  - or npx jest -t "test name" to run a single named test
- If a package.json is later added, prefer using npm scripts (npm run start, npm test, npm run lint).

## 2) High-level architecture (big picture)
- Entry point: index.html (root).
- Main application code: script.js is the single client-side script loaded by index.html.
- No framework or bundler: code runs directly in the browser as plain ES script(s).
- Assets (images/CSS) — not present currently — should be colocated in an `assets/` or `public/` directory and referenced from index.html.

Where to look when editing:
- index.html: markup and script tag(s).
- script.js: DOM behavior and application logic.

## 3) Key conventions and repo-specific notes
- Single-file vanilla JS approach: keep functionality modular inside script.js using functions and namespaced objects to avoid global collisions.
- There is no module system or transpilation step; changes must be compatible with modern browsers (ES6 features are acceptable if target browsers support them).
- No CI or test harness configured — adding tests should include adding a package.json and CI workflow.
- No AI assistant config files detected (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, .cursorrules, .windsurfrules, CONVENTIONS.md, etc.). If introducing automated assistants or CI, add a brief top-level doc referencing their rules.

---

## Helpful hints for Copilot sessions
- Prefer edits that preserve the single-file structure unless a package.json and bundler are introduced.
- When adding dependencies or a test framework, update this file with exact npm scripts and CI workflows so future sessions can run builds/tests reliably.
- If asked to run or validate changes, suggest using a local static server (commands above) and testing in a modern browser.
